solution: 
```cpp
class Solution {
public:
    int integerReplacement(int n) {
        int steps = 0;
        while (n != 1) {
            if (n % 2 == 0) {
                n /= 2;
            } else {
                if ((n - 1) % 4 == 0 && n != 3) {
                    n -= 1;
                } else {
                    n += 1;
                }
            }
            steps++;
        }
        return steps;
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(log n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The algorithm uses a greedy approach to minimize the number of operations by checking if n is even or odd. For odd numbers, it chooses between incrementing or decrementing based on which leads to more even numbers in subsequent steps. This avoids unnecessary steps and ensures optimal performance.
